231→/**
232→ * ### Reviewing - Automatic display answer
233→ *
234→ * * Enabled
235→ * * Time to show answer (20s)
236→ * * Time to show next question (60s)
237→ * * Automatic action: the action to take when reviewing
238→ *
239→ * ### Deck Options - Reviews - Automatic Display Answer
240→ *
241→ * These settings also exist in an options group
242→ * Although they're under "reviews", it counts for all cards
243→ *
244→ * * Use General settings (using the settings above - enabled by default)
245→ * * Enabled, etc...
246→ */
247→class AutomaticAnswerSettings(
248→    val answerAction: AutomaticAnswerAction = AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD,
249→    @get:JvmName("useTimer") val useTimer: Boolean = false,
250→    private val questionDelaySeconds: Int = 60,
251→    private val answerDelaySeconds: Int = 20
252→) {
253→
254→    val questionDelayMilliseconds = questionDelaySeconds * 1000L
255→    val answerDelayMilliseconds = answerDelaySeconds * 1000L
256→
257→    // a wait of zero means auto-advance is disabled
258→    val autoAdvanceAnswer; get() = answerDelaySeconds > 0
259→    val autoAdvanceQuestion; get() = questionDelaySeconds > 0
260→
261→    companion object {
262→        /**
263→         * Obtains the options for [AutomaticAnswer] in the deck config ("review" section)
264→         * @return null if the deck is dynamic (use global settings),
265→         * or if "useGeneralTimeoutSettings" is set
266→         */
267→        fun queryDeckSpecificOptions(
268→            action: AutomaticAnswerAction,
269→            col: Collection,
270→            selectedDid: DeckId
271→        ): AutomaticAnswerSettings? {
272→            // Dynamic don't have review options; attempt to get deck-specific auto-advance options
273→            // but be prepared to go with all default if it's a dynamic deck
274→            if (col.decks.isDyn(selectedDid)) {
275→                return null
276→            }
277→
278→            val revOptions = col.decks.confForDid(selectedDid).getJSONObject("rev")
279→
280→            if (revOptions.optBoolean("useGeneralTimeoutSettings", true)) {
281→                // we want to use the general settings, no need for per-deck settings
282→                return null
283→            }
284→
285→            val useTimer = revOptions.optBoolean("timeoutAnswer", false)
286→            val waitQuestionSecond = revOptions.optInt("timeoutQuestionSeconds", 60)
287→            val waitAnswerSecond = revOptions.optInt("timeoutAnswerSeconds", 20)
288→            return AutomaticAnswerSettings(action, useTimer, waitQuestionSecond, waitAnswerSecond)
289→        }
290→
291→        fun queryFromPreferences(preferences: SharedPreferences, action: AutomaticAnswerAction): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
292→            val prefUseTimer: Boolean = preferences.getBoolean("timeoutAnswer", false)
293→            val prefWaitQuestionSecond: Int = preferences.getInt("timeoutQuestionSeconds", 60)
294→            val prefWaitAnswerSecond: Int = preferences.getInt("timeoutAnswerSeconds", 20)
295→            return AutomaticAnswerSettings(action, prefUseTimer, prefWaitQuestionSecond, prefWaitAnswerSecond)
296→        }
297→
298→        fun createInstance(preferences: SharedPreferences, col: Collection): AutomaticAnswerSettings {
299→            // deck specific options take precedence over general (preference-based) options.
300→            // the action can only be set via preferences (but is stored in the collection).
301→            val action = getAction(col)
302→            return queryDeckSpecificOptions(action, col, col.decks.selected()) ?: queryFromPreferences(preferences, action)
303→        }
304→
305→        private fun getAction(col: Collection): AutomaticAnswerAction {
306→            return try {
307→                val value: Int = col.config.get(AutomaticAnswerAction.CONFIG_KEY) ?: return AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD
308→                AutomaticAnswerAction.fromPreferenceValue(value)
309→            } catch (e: Exception) {
310→                AutomaticAnswerAction.BURY_CARD
311→            }
312→        }
313→    }
314→}
315→
316→/**
317→ * Represents a value from [R.array.automatic_answer_values]/[R.array.automatic_answer_options]
318→ * Executed when answering a card (showing the question).
319→ */
320→enum class AutomaticAnswerAction(private val preferenceValue: Int) {
321→    /** Default: least invasive action */
322→    BURY_CARD(0),
323→    ANSWER_AGAIN(1),
324→    ANSWER_HARD(2),
325→    ANSWER_GOOD(3),
326→    ANSWER_EASY(4);
327→
328→    fun execute(reviewer: Reviewer) {
329→        val numberOfButtons = 4
330→        val actualAction = handleInvalidButtons(numberOfButtons)
331→        val action = actualAction.toCommand(numberOfButtons)
332→        Timber.i("Executing %s", action)
333→        reviewer.executeCommand(action)
334→    }
335→
336→    /** Handle **Hard/Easy** uf they don't appear */
337→    private fun handleInvalidButtons(numberOfButtons: Int): AutomaticAnswerAction {
338→        return when (this) {
339→            ANSWER_HARD -> if (AnswerButtons.canAnswerHard(numberOfButtons)) ANSWER_HARD else ANSWER_GOOD
340→            ANSWER_EASY -> if (AnswerButtons.canAnswerEasy(numberOfButtons)) ANSWER_EASY else ANSWER_GOOD
341→            // Again and Good always appear. So does Bury
342→            else -> this
343→        }
344→    }
345→
346→    /** Convert to a [ViewerCommand] */
347→    private fun toCommand(numberOfButtons: Int): ViewerCommand {
348→        return when (this) {
349→            BURY_CARD -> ViewerCommand.BURY_CARD
350→            ANSWER_AGAIN -> AGAIN.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
351→            ANSWER_HARD -> HARD.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
352→            ANSWER_GOOD -> GOOD.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
353→            ANSWER_EASY -> EASY.toViewerCommand(numberOfButtons)
354→        }
355→    }
356→
357→    companion object {
358→        /**
359→         * An integer representing the action when Automatic Answer flips a card from answer to question
360→         *
361→         * 0 represents "bury", 1-4 represents the named buttons
362→         *
363→         * Although AnkiMobile and AnkiDroid have the feature, this config key is currently AnkiDroid only
364→         *
365→         * @see AutomaticAnswerAction
366→         */
367→        const val CONFIG_KEY = "automaticAnswerAction"
368→
369→        /** convert from [R.array.automatic_answer_values] ([R.array.automatic_answer_options]) to the enum */
370→        fun fromPreferenceValue(i: Int): AutomaticAnswerAction {
371→            return values().firstOrNull { it.preferenceValue == i } ?: BURY_CARD
372→        }
373→    }
374→}

package com.ichi2.libanki

import anki.import_export.ExportAnkiPackageOptions
import anki.import_export.ExportLimit
import anki.search.SearchNode
import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend

/**
 * (Maybe) create a colpkg backup, while keeping the collection open. If the
 * configured backup interval has not elapsed, and force=false, no backup will be created,
 * and this routine will return false.
 *
 * There must not be an active transaction.
 *
 * If `waitForCompletion` is true, block until the backup completes. Otherwise this routine
 * returns quickly, and the backup can be awaited on a background thread with awaitBackupCompletion()
 * to check for success.
 *
 * Backups are automatically expired according to the user's settings.
 *
 */
fun Collection.createBackup(
    backupFolder: String,
    force: Boolean,
    waitForCompletion: Boolean
): Boolean {
    return backend.createBackup(
        backupFolder = backupFolder,
        force = force,
        waitForCompletion = waitForCompletion
    )
}

/**
 * If a backup is running, block until it completes, throwing if it fails, or already
 * failed, and the status has not yet been checked. On failure, an error is only returned
 * once; subsequent calls are a no-op until another backup is run.
 */
fun Collection.awaitBackupCompletion() {
    backend.awaitBackupCompletion()
}

/**
 * Replace the collection file with the one in the provided .colpkg file.
 * The collection must be already closed, and must be opened afterwards.
 * */
fun importCollectionPackage(
    backend: Backend,
    colPath: String,
    colpkgPath: String
) {
    backend.importCollectionPackage(
        colPath = colPath,
        backupPath = colpkgPath,
        mediaFolder = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media"),
        mediaDb = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media.db")
    )
}

/**
 * Export the collection into a .colpkg file.
 * If legacy=false, a file targeting Anki 2.1.50+ is created. It compresses better and is faster to
 * create, but older clients can not read it.
 */
fun Collection.exportCollectionPackage(
    outPath: String,
    includeMedia: Boolean,
    legacy: Boolean = true
) {
    close(forFullSync = true)
    backend.exportCollectionPackage(
        outPath = outPath,
        includeMedia = includeMedia,
        legacy = legacy
    )
    reopen()
}

fun Collection.importAnkiPackageRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
    return backend.importAnkiPackageRaw(input)
}

/**
 * Export the specified deck to an .apkg file.
 * * If legacy is false, an apkg will be created that can only
 * be opened with recent Anki versions.
 */
fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(
    outPath: String,
    withScheduling: Boolean,
    withMedia: Boolean,
    limit: ExportLimit,
    legacy: Boolean = true
) {
    val options = ExportAnkiPackageOptions
        .newBuilder()
        .setWithScheduling(withScheduling)
        .setWithMedia(withMedia)
        .setLegacy(legacy)
        // required by Anki 23.12+ import UI
        .setWithDeckConfigs(true)
        .build()
    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, options, limit)
}

fun Collection.exportNotesCsv(
    outPath: String,
    withHtml: Boolean,
    withTags: Boolean,
    withDeck: Boolean,
    withNotetype: Boolean,
    withGuid: Boolean,
    limit: ExportLimit
) {
    backend.exportNoteCsv(outPath, withHtml, withTags, withDeck, withNotetype, withGuid, limit)
}

fun Collection.exportCardsCsv(outPath: String, withHtml: Boolean, limit: ExportLimit) {
    backend.exportCardCsv(outPath, withHtml, limit)
}

fun Collection.getCsvMetadataRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
    return backend.getCsvMetadataRaw(input)
}
